summary: Add an OpenAI-compatible LLM client boundary plus safe JSON parse, Zod validation, and one-step repair flow.
acceptance_criteria: A reusable LLM client interface exists with typed chat/completion input, output, and error surfaces suitable for an OpenAI-compatible provider.

An OpenAI-compatible client adapter accepts explicit configuration such as baseUrl, apiKey, model, and optional fetch implementation, fails fast on missing configuration, and does not read process.env directly.

safeJsonCompletion() parses assistant JSON, validates it with a caller-provided Zod schema, attempts at most one repair call after parse or validation failure, and returns structured success/failure results without crashing the caller on malformed model output.
JSON extraction handles plain JSON and common fenced JSON responses without accepting unrelated prose as valid output.
Focused automated tests cover valid JSON, parse failure with successful repair, validation or repair failure without throwing, and OpenAI-compatible request shape using a fake transport only.
README documents the LLM JSON utility slice, configuration boundary, no-secret/no-network test policy, and the test command.
No real LLM call, environment-variable secret loading, agent implementation, scan orchestration, database write, API route, UI, auth, cron, deployment, or paid service setup is implemented.
notes: Derived from docs/08_ai_agents_and_prompts.md JSON parsing strategy and docs/11_development_plan.md recommended step 6 after SERP provider/mock provider.
systems_architect is required because this creates the LLM service boundary used by later agents.
security_reviewer is required because the boundary will later carry API keys and external network calls; this task must avoid direct secret loading and real calls.
Keep this task limited to the reusable LLM client boundary and JSON repair utility. Keyword, SERP, opportunity, MVP spec agents and scan orchestration belong to later tasks.
non_goals: No Keyword Expansion Agent, SERP Analysis Agent, Opportunity Analysis Agent, or MVP Spec Agent.
No SearchRun, KeywordCandidate, SerpResult, Opportunity, or MvpSpec persistence.
No process.env reads, .env changes, real API keys, real network calls in tests, or paid service setup.
No dashboard, radar task UI, auth, cron, deployment, or public release work.
No changes to the existing SERP provider contract.
